title: FERPA - Family Education Rights and Privacy Act
content:
FERPA (Family Education Rights and Privacy Act)
Morgan State University - Office of the Registrar

This guide is for STUDENTS. It explains how to submit a FERPA release to grant or revoke access to your educational records.

---

STEP 1: UNDERSTAND FERPA
FERPA protects the privacy of student education records for students aged 18 and older. It is a federal law enforced by the U.S. Department of Education.

Learn more about FERPA on the U.S. Department of Education website.
Learn about Morgan State University's application of the FERPA policy.

Through FERPA, you can grant or revoke access to the following information:
- Class schedule
- Grades
- Financial aid
- Billing information

Access is managed through Campus ESP at morgan.campusesp.com.

IMPORTANT: Only the student can submit a FERPA release. No one else can submit it on your behalf.

To revoke access: Contact records@morgan.edu.

---

STEP 2: ENTER YOUR INFORMATION
Enter your name and federal tax status:
- Dependent: Yes
- Not a dependent: No
- Unsure: I don't know

---

STEP 3: CHECK CONSENT
Review and check the consent box. Your authorization remains in effect until you submit a written revocation to the Office of the Registrar.

---

STEP 4: ENTER AUTHORIZED PERSONS
Enter the full legal name of the person(s) you are granting access to your information. You can add additional persons if needed.

---

STEP 5: SUBMIT
Submit the form. Processing takes up to 3 business days.

For the full official guide with screenshots, view: [FERPA Guide](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1IGdgVnKAh-CudkTQRNgSm6UgXGSTWOV0/view)
